lines.speaker: FRANCE
lines.text: Come, my fair Cordelia.
France and Cordelia exit .

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Sister, it is not little I have to say of what

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: most nearly appertains to us both. I think our

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: father will hence tonight.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: That’s most certain, and with you; next month

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: with us.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: You see how full of changes his age is; the

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: observation we have made of it hath not been

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: little. He always loved our sister most, and with

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: what poor judgment he hath now cast her off

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: appears too grossly.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: ’Tis the infirmity of his age. Yet he hath ever

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: but slenderly known himself.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: The best and soundest of his time hath been

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: but rash. Then must we look from his age to

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: receive not alone the imperfections of long-engraffed

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: condition, but therewithal the unruly waywardness

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: that infirm and choleric years bring with

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: them.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Such unconstant starts are we like to have

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: from him as this of Kent’s banishment.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: There is further compliment of leave-taking

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: between France and him. Pray you, let us sit

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: together. If our father carry authority with such

lines.speaker: GONERIL

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: but offend us.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: We shall further think of it.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: We must do something, and i’ th’ heat.
They exit .

scenes.synopsis: Lear divides his kingdom among his daughters based on declarations of love; Cordelia refuses to flatter him and is disinherited, and Kent is banished for defending her. France takes Cordelia as his queen.

lines.text: Enter Edmund , the Bastard .

lines.text: My services are bound. Wherefore should I

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Stand in the plague of custom, and permit

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: The curiosity of nations to deprive me

lines.text: For that I am some twelve or fourteen moonshines

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Lag of a brother? why bastard? Wherefore base,

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.text: Go to th’ creating a whole tribe of fops

Enter Gloucester .

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Kent banished thus? And France in choler parted?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

He puts a paper in his pocket .

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: letter?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I know no news, my lord.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: What paper were you reading?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Nothing, my lord.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: No? What needed then that terrible dispatch

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: of it into your pocket? The quality of nothing

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: hath not such need to hide itself. Let’s see. Come, if

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: it be nothing, I shall not need spectacles.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I beseech you, sir, pardon me. It is a letter

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: from my brother that I have not all o’erread; and

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: for so much as I have perused, I find it not fit for

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: your o’erlooking.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Give me the letter, sir.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I shall offend either to detain or give it. The

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: contents, as in part I understand them, are to

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: blame.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Let’s see, let’s see.
Edmund gives him the paper .

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I hope, for my brother’s justification, he

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: wrote this but as an essay or taste of my virtue.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: This policy and reverence of age
( reads )

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: makes the world bitter to the best of our times, keeps

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: our fortunes from us till our oldness cannot relish

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: them. I begin to find an idle and fond bondage in the

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: oppression of aged tyranny, who sways not as it hath

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: power but as it is suffered. Come to me, that of this I

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: him, you should enjoy half his revenue forever and

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: live the beloved of your brother. Edgar.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Hum? Conspiracy? Sleep till I wake him, you

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: should enjoy half his revenue. My son Edgar! Had

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: cunning of it. I found it thrown in at the casement

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: of my closet.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: brother’s?

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: It is his.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: It is his hand, my lord, but I hope his heart is

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: not in the contents.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Has he never before sounded you in this

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: business?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Never, my lord. But I have heard him oft

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: maintain it to be fit that, sons at perfect age and

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: fathers declined, the father should be as ward to the

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: O villain, villain! His very opinion in the

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: letter. Abhorred villain! Unnatural, detested, brutish

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: villain! Worse than brutish!—Go, sirrah, seek

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: him. I’ll apprehend him.—Abominable villain!—

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Where is he?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I do not well know, my lord. If it shall please

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: you to suspend your indignation against my brother

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: intent, you should run a certain course; where, if

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: purpose, it would make a great gap in your own

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I dare pawn down my life for him that he hath

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: writ this to feel my affection to your Honor, and to

lines.text: Think you so?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: If your Honor judge it meet, I will place you

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: where you shall hear us confer of this, and by an

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: auricular assurance have your satisfaction, and that

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: without any further delay than this very evening.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: He cannot be such a monster.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: To his father, that so tenderly and entirely

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: loves him! Heaven and Earth! Edmund, seek him

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: out; wind me into him, I pray you. Frame the

lines.text: I will seek him, sir, presently, convey the

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: business as I shall find means, and acquaint you

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: withal.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: These late eclipses in the sun and moon

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: portend no good to us. Though the wisdom of

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: nature can reason it thus and thus, yet nature finds

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: itself scourged by the sequent effects. Love cools,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: against father. The King falls from bias of nature:

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: lose thee nothing. Do it carefully.—And the noble

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: ’Tis strange.
He exits .

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.speaker: EDGAR

lines.speaker: EDGAR

lines.text: scarcely allay.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Some villain hath done me wrong.

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: and, as I say, retire with me to my lodging, from

lines.text: Pray you go. There’s my key. If you do stir abroad,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Armed, brother?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Brother, I advise you to the best. I am no

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: honest man if there be any good meaning toward

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: you. I have told you what I have seen and heard, but

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: faintly, nothing like the image and horror of it. Pray

lines.speaker: EDMUND

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Shall I hear from you anon?

lines.speaker: EDMUND

Edgar exits .

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: A credulous father and a brother noble,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Whose nature is so far from doing harms

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: That he suspects none; on whose foolish honesty

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: My practices ride easy. I see the business.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Let me, if not by birth, have lands by wit.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: All with me’s meet that I can fashion fit.

scenes.synopsis: Edmund tricks his father Gloucester into believing his legitimate brother Edgar is plotting against him, and separately convinces Edgar he is in danger from their father.

lines.text: Enter Goneril and Oswald , her Steward .

lines.text: If he distaste it, let him to my sister,

lines.text: Whose mind and mine I know in that are one,

lines.text: That still would manage those authorities

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: That he hath given away. Now, by my life,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: With checks as flatteries, when they are seen

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: abused.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Remember what I have said.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Well, madam.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: And let his knights have colder looks among you.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: What grows of it, no matter. Advise your fellows so.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: I would breed from hence occasions, and I shall,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: That I may speak. I’ll write straight to my sister

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: To hold my very course. Prepare for dinner.

scenes.synopsis: Goneril, irritated by Lear's riotous knights, instructs her steward Oswald to treat Lear and his men with deliberate disrespect.

lines.text: Enter Kent in disguise .

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: If but as well I other accents borrow

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: That can my speech diffuse, my good intent

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: May carry through itself to that full issue

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: For which I razed my likeness. Now, banished Kent,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: If thou canst serve where thou dost stand

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: condemned,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: So may it come thy master, whom thou lov’st,

Horns within . Enter Lear , Knights , and Attendants .

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Let me not stay a jot for dinner. Go get it ready.
An Attendant exits .

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: How now, what art thou?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: A man, sir.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: What dost thou profess? What wouldst thou with

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: us?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: I do profess to be no less than I seem, to serve

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: him truly that will put me in trust, to love him that

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: is honest, to converse with him that is wise and says

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: little, to fear judgment, to fight when I cannot

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: choose, and to eat no fish.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: What art thou?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: A very honest-hearted fellow, and as poor as the

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: King.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: If thou be’st as poor for a subject as he’s for a

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: king, thou art poor enough. What wouldst thou?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Service.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Who wouldst thou serve?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: You.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Dost thou know me, fellow?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: No, sir, but you have that in your countenance

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: which I would fain call master.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: What’s that?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Authority.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: What services canst do?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: I can keep honest counsel, ride, run, mar a

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: curious tale in telling it, and deliver a plain message

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: bluntly. That which ordinary men are fit for I

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: am qualified in, and the best of me is diligence.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: How old art thou?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Not so young, sir, to love a woman for singing,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: nor so old to dote on her for anything. I have years

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: on my back forty-eight.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Follow me. Thou shalt serve me—if I like thee

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: no worse after dinner. I will not part from thee

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: yet.—Dinner, ho, dinner!—Where’s my knave, my

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Fool? Go you and call my Fool hither.

Enter Oswald , the Steward .

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: You, you, sirrah, where’s my daughter?

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: So please you—

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: What says the fellow there? Call the clotpole

lines.text: Though I condemn not, yet, under pardon,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: You are much more at task for want of wisdom

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Than praised for harmful mildness.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: How far your eyes may pierce I cannot tell.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Striving to better, oft we mar what’s well.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Nay, then—

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Well, well, th’ event.
They exit .

scenes.location: A hall in The Duke of Albany's palace
scenes.synopsis: Kent returns in disguise and enters Lear's service; the Fool makes his first appearance and mocks Lear's folly. Goneril confronts Lear and demands he halve his retinue; Lear curses her and departs for Regan.

lines.text: Enter Lear , Kent in disguise , Gentleman , and Fool .

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Go you before to Gloucester with these
to Kent

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: letters. Acquaint my daughter no further with anything

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: you know than comes from her demand out of

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: the letter. If your diligence be not speedy, I shall be

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: there afore you.

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: I will not sleep, my lord, till I have delivered

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: your letter.
He exits .

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: If a man’s brains were in ’s heels, were ’t not in

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: danger of kibes?

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Ay, boy.

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Then, I prithee, be merry; thy wit shall not go

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: slipshod.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Ha, ha, ha!

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Shalt see thy other daughter will use thee kindly,

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: for, though she’s as like this as a crab’s like an

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: apple, yet I can tell what I can tell.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: What canst tell, boy?

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: She will taste as like this as a crab does to a crab.

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Thou canst tell why one’s nose stands i’ th’ middle

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: on ’s face?

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Why, to keep one’s eyes of either side ’s nose,

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: that what a man cannot smell out he may spy into.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: I did her wrong.

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Canst tell how an oyster makes his shell?

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Nor I neither. But I can tell why a snail has a

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: house.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Why, to put ’s head in, not to give it away to his

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: daughters and leave his horns without a case.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: I will forget my nature. So kind a father!—Be

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: my horses ready?
Gentleman exits .

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Thy asses are gone about ’em. The reason why

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: the seven stars are no more than seven is a pretty

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: reason.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Because they are not eight.

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Yes, indeed. Thou wouldst make a good Fool.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: To take ’t again perforce! Monster ingratitude!

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: If thou wert my Fool, nuncle, I’d have thee

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: beaten for being old before thy time.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: How’s that?

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Thou shouldst not have been old till thou hadst

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: been wise.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: O, let me not be mad, not mad, sweet heaven!

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Keep me in temper. I would not be mad!
Enter Gentleman .

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: How now, are the horses ready?

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Ready, my lord.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Come, boy.

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: She that’s a maid now and laughs at my departure,

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: Shall not be a maid long, unless things be cut

lines.speaker: FOOL
lines.text: shorter.
They exit .

scenes.location: Before the Duke of Albany's palace
scenes.synopsis: Lear sends Kent ahead with a letter to Regan; the Fool continues to taunt Lear about his foolishness, and Lear begins to fear for his sanity.

lines.text: Enter Edmund , the Bastard and Curan , severally .

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Save thee, Curan.

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: And you, sir. I have been with your father and

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: given him notice that the Duke of Cornwall and

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: Regan his duchess will be here with him this night.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: How comes that?

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: Nay, I know not. You have heard of the news

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: abroad?—I mean the whispered ones, for they are

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: yet but ear-kissing arguments.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Not I. Pray you, what are they?

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: Have you heard of no likely wars toward ’twixt

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: the dukes of Cornwall and Albany?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Not a word.

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: You may do, then, in time. Fare you well, sir.
He exits .

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: The Duke be here tonight? The better, best.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: This weaves itself perforce into my business.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: My father hath set guard to take my brother,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: And I have one thing of a queasy question

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Which I must act. Briefness and fortune work!—

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Brother, a word. Descend. Brother, I say!
Enter Edgar .

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: My father watches. O sir, fly this place!

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Intelligence is given where you are hid.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: You have now the good advantage of the night.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Have you not spoken ’gainst the Duke of Cornwall?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: He’s coming hither, now, i’ th’ night, i’ th’ haste,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: And Regan with him. Have you nothing said

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Upon his party ’gainst the Duke of Albany?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Advise yourself.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I am sure on ’t, not a word.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I hear my father coming. Pardon me.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: In cunning I must draw my sword upon you.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Draw. Seem to defend yourself. Now, quit you

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: well.
They draw .

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Yield! Come before my father! Light, hoa, here!

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Fly, brother.—Torches, torches!
Aside to Edgar .

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: —So, farewell.
Edgar exits .

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Some blood drawn on me would beget opinion

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Of my more fierce endeavor. I have seen drunkards

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Do more than this in sport.
He wounds his arm .

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Father, father!

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Stop, stop! No help?
Enter Gloucester , and Servants with torches .

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Now, Edmund, where’s the

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: villain?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Here stood he in the dark, his sharp sword out,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Mumbling of wicked charms, conjuring the moon

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: To stand auspicious mistress.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: But where is he?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Look, sir, I bleed.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Where is the villain,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Edmund?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Fled this way, sir, when by no means he could—

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Pursue him, ho! Go after. By no
Servants exit .

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: means what?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Persuade me to the murder of your Lordship,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: But that I told him the revenging gods

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: ’Gainst parricides did all the thunder bend,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Spoke with how manifold and strong a bond

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: The child was bound to th’ father—sir, in fine,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Seeing how loathly opposite I stood

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: To his unnatural purpose, in fell motion

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: With his preparèd sword he charges home

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: My unprovided body, lanced mine arm;

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: And when he saw my best alarumed spirits,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Bold in the quarrel’s right, roused to th’ encounter,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Or whether ghasted by the noise I made,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Full suddenly he fled.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Let him fly far!

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Not in this land shall he remain uncaught,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: And found—dispatch. The noble duke my master,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: My worthy arch and patron, comes tonight.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: By his authority I will proclaim it

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: That he which finds him shall deserve our thanks,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Bringing the murderous coward to the stake;

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: He that conceals him, death.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: When I dissuaded him from his intent

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: And found him pight to do it, with curst speech

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I threatened to discover him. He replied

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Thou unpossessing bastard, dost thou think

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: If I would stand against thee, would the reposal

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Of any trust, virtue, or worth in thee

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Make thy words faithed? No. What I should

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: deny—

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: As this I would, though thou didst produce

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: My very character—I’d turn it all

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: To thy suggestion, plot, and damnèd practice.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: And thou must make a dullard of the world

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: If they not thought the profits of my death

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Were very pregnant and potential spurs

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: To make thee seek it.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: O strange and fastened villain!

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Would he deny his letter, said he?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I never got him.
Tucket within .

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Hark, the Duke’s trumpets. I know not why he

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: comes.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: All ports I’ll bar. The villain shall not ’scape.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: The Duke must grant me that. Besides, his picture

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I will send far and near, that all the kingdom

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: May have due note of him. And of my land,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Loyal and natural boy, I’ll work the means

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: To make thee capable.
Enter Cornwall , Regan , and Attendants .

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: How now, my noble friend? Since I came hither,

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Which I can call but now, I have heard strange

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: news.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: If it be true, all vengeance comes too short

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Which can pursue th’ offender. How dost, my

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: lord?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: O madam, my old heart is cracked; it’s cracked.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: What, did my father’s godson seek your life?

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: He whom my father named, your Edgar?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: O lady, lady, shame would have it hid!

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Was he not companion with the riotous knights

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: That tended upon my father?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I know not, madam. ’Tis too bad, too bad.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Yes, madam, he was of that consort.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: No marvel, then, though he were ill affected.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: ’Tis they have put him on the old man’s death,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: To have th’ expense and waste of his revenues.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: I have this present evening from my sister

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Been well informed of them, and with such cautions

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: That if they come to sojourn at my house

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: I’ll not be there.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Nor I, assure thee, Regan.—

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Edmund, I hear that you have shown your father

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: A childlike office.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: It was my duty, sir.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: He did bewray his practice, and received

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: This hurt you see striving to apprehend him.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Is he pursued?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Ay, my good lord.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: If he be taken, he shall never more

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Be feared of doing harm. Make your own purpose,

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: How in my strength you please.—For you, Edmund,

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Whose virtue and obedience doth this instant

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: So much commend itself, you shall be ours.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Natures of such deep trust we shall much need.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: You we first seize on.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I shall serve you, sir,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Truly, however else.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: For him I thank your Grace.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: You know not why we came to visit you—

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Thus out of season, threading dark-eyed night.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Occasions, noble Gloucester, of some poise,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Wherein we must have use of your advice.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Our father he hath writ, so hath our sister,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Of differences, which I best thought it fit

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: To answer from our home. The several messengers

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: From hence attend dispatch. Our good old friend,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Lay comforts to your bosom and bestow

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Your needful counsel to our businesses,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Which craves the instant use.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I serve you, madam.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Your Graces are right welcome.
Flourish . They exit .

scenes.synopsis: Edmund stages a false confrontation with Edgar to convince Gloucester of Edgar's treachery; Edgar flees and is outlawed. Cornwall and Regan arrive and take Edmund into their service.

lines.text: Enter Kent in disguise and Oswald , the Steward , severally .

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Good dawning to thee, friend. Art of this

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: house?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Ay.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Where may we set our horses?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: I’ th’ mire.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Prithee, if thou lov’st me, tell me.

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: I love thee not.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Why then, I care not for thee.

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: If I had thee in Lipsbury pinfold, I would make

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: thee care for me.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Why dost thou use me thus? I know thee not.

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Fellow, I know thee.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: What dost thou know me for?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: A knave, a rascal, an eater of broken meats; a

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: base, proud, shallow, beggarly, three-suited, hundred-pound,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: filthy worsted-stocking knave; a lily-livered,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: action-taking, whoreson, glass-gazing, superserviceable,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: finical rogue; one-trunk-inheriting

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: slave; one that wouldst be a bawd in way of good

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: service, and art nothing but the composition of a

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: knave, beggar, coward, pander, and the son and heir

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: of a mongrel bitch; one whom I will beat into

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: clamorous whining if thou deny’st the least syllable

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: of thy addition.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Why, what a monstrous fellow art thou thus

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: to rail on one that is neither known of thee nor

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: knows thee!

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: What a brazen-faced varlet art thou to deny thou

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: knowest me! Is it two days ago since I tripped up

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: thy heels and beat thee before the King?
He draws his sword .

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Draw, you rogue, for though it be night,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: yet the moon shines. I’ll make a sop o’ th’ moonshine

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: of you, you whoreson, cullionly barbermonger.

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Draw!

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Away! I have nothing to do with thee.

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Draw, you rascal! You come with letters against

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: the King and take Vanity the puppet’s part against

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: the royalty of her father. Draw, you rogue, or I’ll so

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: carbonado your shanks! Draw, you rascal! Come

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: your ways.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Help, ho! Murder! Help!

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Strike, you slave! Stand, rogue! Stand, you neat

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: slave! Strike!
He beats Oswald .

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Help, ho! Murder, murder!
Enter Bastard Edmund , with his rapier drawn , Cornwall , Regan , Gloucester , Servants .

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: How now, what’s the matter? Part!

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: With you, goodman boy, if you please. Come, I’ll

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: flesh you. Come on, young master.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Weapons? Arms? What’s the matter here?

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Keep peace, upon your lives! He dies that

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: strikes again. What is the matter?

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: The messengers from our sister and the King.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: What is your difference? Speak.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: I am scarce in breath, my lord.

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: No marvel, you have so bestirred your valor.

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: You cowardly rascal, nature disclaims in thee; a

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: tailor made thee.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Thou art a strange fellow. A tailor make a

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: man?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: A tailor, sir. A stonecutter or a painter could not

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: have made him so ill, though they had been but two

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: years o’ th’ trade.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Speak yet, how grew your quarrel?

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: This ancient ruffian, sir, whose life I have

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: spared at suit of his gray beard—

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Thou whoreson zed, thou unnecessary letter!

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: —My lord, if you will give me leave, I will tread

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: this unbolted villain into mortar and daub the wall

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: of a jakes with him.—Spare my gray beard, you

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: wagtail?

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Peace, sirrah!

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: You beastly knave, know you no reverence?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Yes, sir, but anger hath a privilege.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Why art thou angry?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: That such a slave as this should wear a sword,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Who wears no honesty. Such smiling rogues as

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: these,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Like rats, oft bite the holy cords atwain

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Which are too intrinse t’ unloose; smooth every

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: passion

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: That in the natures of their lords rebel—

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Being oil to fire, snow to the colder moods—

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Renege, affirm, and turn their halcyon beaks

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: With every gale and vary of their masters,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Knowing naught, like dogs, but following.—

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: A plague upon your epileptic visage!

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Smile you my speeches, as I were a fool?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Goose, if I had you upon Sarum plain,

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: I’d drive you cackling home to Camelot.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: What, art thou mad, old fellow?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: How fell you out? Say that.

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: No contraries hold more antipathy

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: Than I and such a knave.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Why dost thou call him knave? What is his fault?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: His countenance likes me not.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL

scenes.synopsis: Lear finds Kent in the stocks and is progressively humiliated as both Regan and Goneril refuse to house any of his knights. Stripped of his retinue, Lear rushes out into a gathering storm.

scenes.synopsis: A storm rages; Kent learns from a Gentleman that Lear is wandering the heath with only his Fool, and that there is a secret rift between Albany and Cornwall. Kent sends the Gentleman to Dover to alert Cordelia.

scenes.synopsis: Gloucester confides to Edmund that he has received a letter about a French invasion and intends to help Lear; Edmund immediately resolves to betray him to Cornwall.

scenes.synopsis: Lear and the Fool take shelter in a hovel and encounter Edgar disguised as Poor Tom; Lear, fascinated by the supposed mad beggar, begins to strip off his clothes. Gloucester arrives to guide them to safety.

scenes.synopsis: Edmund delivers Gloucester's treasonous letter to Cornwall, who resolves to arrest Gloucester; Edmund is made the new Earl of Gloucester.

scenes.synopsis: In a farmhouse shelter, Lear stages a mock trial of his daughters using Edgar, the Fool, and Kent as judges; his madness is now fully apparent. Gloucester warns Kent to flee with Lear toward Dover.

scenes.synopsis: Cornwall, urged on by Regan, interrogates Gloucester and has his eyes put out; a servant fatally wounds Cornwall before being killed. Gloucester, blinded, is thrust out and told to smell his way to Dover.

scenes.synopsis: Edgar encounters his blind, wandering father Gloucester, who does not recognise him; Gloucester asks to be guided to Dover, where he intends to end his life at the cliffs.

scenes.location: Before the Duke of Albany's palace
scenes.synopsis: Goneril and Edmund arrive at Albany's palace; Albany confronts Goneril over the treatment of Lear and the blinding of Gloucester. News arrives that Cornwall has died from his wound.

scenes.synopsis: Kent learns from a Gentleman that Cordelia has received his letters and wept; Lear, ashamed, refuses to see her even though she is nearby.

lines.text: Enter with Drum and Colors , Cordelia , Doctor , Gentlemen , and Soldiers .

scenes.synopsis: Cordelia sends soldiers to find Lear, who has been seen wandering crowned with wild flowers; a messenger brings word that the British armies are advancing.

lines.text: Enter Regan and Oswald , the Steward .

scenes.synopsis: Regan questions Oswald about Goneril's letters to Edmund, revealing her own interest in him; she asks Oswald to kill Gloucester if he finds him.

lines.text: Enter Gloucester and Edgar dressed as a peasant .

scenes.synopsis: Edgar leads blind Gloucester to what he pretends is Dover cliff; Gloucester attempts suicide but survives. Lear enters raving in a wild flower crown; Edgar kills Oswald and discovers Goneril's treasonous letter to Edmund.

lines.text: Methinks I should know you and know this man,

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Yet I am doubtful, for I am mainly ignorant

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: What place this is, and all the skill I have

lines.text: Remembers not these garments; nor I know not

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Where I did lodge last night. Do not laugh at me,

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: For, as I am a man, I think this lady

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: To be my child Cordelia.

lines.speaker: CORDELIA
lines.text: And so I am; I am.
weeping

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Be your tears wet? Yes, faith. I pray, weep not.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: If you have poison for me, I will drink it.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: I know you do not love me, for your sisters

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Have, as I do remember, done me wrong.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: You have some cause; they have not.

lines.speaker: CORDELIA
lines.text: No cause, no

lines.speaker: CORDELIA
lines.text: cause.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Am I in France?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: In your own kingdom, sir.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Do not abuse me.

lines.text: Be comforted, good madam. The great rage,

lines.speaker: DOCTOR

lines.speaker: DOCTOR

lines.speaker: DOCTOR
lines.text: Till further settling.

lines.speaker: CORDELIA
lines.text: Will ’t please your Highness walk?

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: You must bear with me.

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: Pray you now, forget, and forgive. I am old and

lines.speaker: LEAR
lines.text: foolish.
They exit . Kent and Gentleman remain .

lines.text: Holds it true, sir, that the Duke of Cornwall

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: was so slain?

lines.speaker: KENT
lines.text: As ’tis said, the bastard son of Gloucester.

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: the Earl of Kent in Germany.

lines.text: you well, sir.
He exits .

lines.speaker: KENT

lines.speaker: KENT

scenes.location: A tent in the French camp near Dover
scenes.synopsis: Cordelia is reunited with a sleeping Lear; he wakes, confused and penitent, and she comforts him. A battle against the British forces is imminent.

lines.text: Enter , with Drum and Colors , Edmund , Regan , Gentlemen , and Soldiers .

lines.text: Let’s then determine

lines.text: With th’ ancient of war on our proceeding.

lines.text: Sister, you’ll go with us?

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: ’Tis most convenient. Pray, go with us.

lines.speaker: GONERIL

aside
They begin to exit .
Enter Edgar dressed as a peasant .

lines.text: The enemy’s in view. Draw up your powers.

lines.text: Each jealous of the other as the stung

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Her husband being alive. Now, then, we’ll use

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: His countenance for the battle, which, being done,

lines.text: Let her who would be rid of him devise

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Which he intends to Lear and to Cordelia,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: The battle done and they within our power,

lines.speaker: EDMUND

scenes.synopsis: Edmund and Albany prepare for battle; Edgar, still disguised, gives Albany Goneril's treasonous letter. Edmund privately resolves to show no mercy to Lear and Cordelia.

scenes.location: A field between the two camps
scenes.synopsis: Battle is joined and quickly over; the French forces are defeated and Lear and Cordelia are captured.

lines.speaker: LEAR

lines.speaker: LEAR

scenes.synopsis: Edmund sends Lear and Cordelia to prison with a secret order for their execution. Edgar mortally wounds Edmund; Goneril poisons Regan and kills herself. The death order comes too late — Cordelia has been hanged. Lear enters carrying her body and dies of grief.

synopsis: King Lear dramatizes the story of an aged king of ancient Britain, whose plan to divide his kingdom among his three daughters ends tragically. When he tests each by asking how much she loves him, the older daughters, Goneril and Regan, flatter him. The youngest, Cordelia, does not, and Lear disowns and banishes her. She marries the king of France. Goneril and Regan turn on Lear, leaving him to wander madly in a furious storm. Meanwhile, the Earl of Gloucester's illegitimate son Edmund turns Gloucester against his legitimate son, Edgar. Gloucester, appalled at the daughters' treatment of Lear, gets news that a French army is coming to help Lear. Edmund betrays Gloucester to Regan and her husband, Cornwall, who puts out Gloucester's eyes and makes Edmund the Earl of Gloucester. Cordelia and the French army save Lear, but the army is defeated. Edmund imprisons Cordelia and Lear. Edgar then mortally wounds Edmund in a trial by combat. Dying, Edmund confesses that he has ordered the deaths of Cordelia and Lear. Before they can be rescued, Lear brings in Cordelia's body and then he himself dies.
